sub_tasks.requirements: Method and code fidelity to the paper

Implements Audio Token Selection using the last layer of the audio encoder: computes A = Softmax(QK^T/√d) (Eq. 2), derives the per-token mean-attention score vector a_avg over all other audio tokens, applies the same average-pooling used to pool audio tokens to keep index alignment, and selects the top ρa% as salient tokens, as described in Section 3.3.
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: Implements the audio-guided dynamic video pruning rate ρ'_v(i) = ρmax − (ρmax − ρmin)·Sa(i) (Eq. 5) with ρmax=0.75 and ρmin=0.35, where high audio-retention windows are pruned conservatively and low-retention windows aggressively, then normalizes ρ'_v(i) so final ρv adheres to the global pruning budget (Section 3.3, Section 4.1).
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: Implements Audio Anchor Consolidation: uniformly samples anchors from non-salient audio tokens, computes cross-modal similarity S_cross = Ĥa Ĥv^⊺ with S_ij ∈ [−1,1] (Eq. 3) using the normalization Ĥ = Diag(√diag(HH^⊺)+ε)^−1 H with ε=1e−6 (Eq. 4), and merges the top-G audio tokens into each anchor with G=15 for AVUT and G=3 for other benchmarks (Section 3.3, Section 4.1).
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: Implements the ISTC block with a minimum processing unit of four frames: computes inter-frame cosine similarity S_vid (Eq. 6) to temporally merge/prune high-similarity tokens in frames 2 and 4, and applies DPC-KNN density-peak clustering on frames 1 and 3 using local density ρi (Eq. 7) and distance δi (Eq. 8) with k=5, pruning by the δi×ρi density score (Section 3.4, Section 4.1).
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: Re-implements all comparison baselines on the same backbone: Random pruning (control), FastV using the L-th layer attention matrix with the attention-computation layer set to layer 5, and DyCoke (V&A) applying its first-stage TTM module to both audio and video tokens; the VisionZip global-selection (GS) baseline is included for the ablation (Section 4.1).
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: Data and preprocessing fidelity

Evaluates on the four named audio-video benchmarks: AVUT/AVUTBench (six tasks event localization EL, object matching OM, OCR matching OR, information extraction IE, content counting CC, character matching CM), VideoMME, ShortVid-Bench, and WorldSense (eight domains), as listed in Section 4.1.
Applies the paper's frame caps: maximum 768 frames for the relatively long VideoMME videos and 128 frames for all other datasets, as specified in Section 4.1 Implementation Details.

Constructs the audio-video token sequence with fixed-length time windows containing 50 audio tokens and 288 video tokens per window, with co-temporal tokens aligned and concatenated into cross-modal blocks (Section 3.1, Section 4.1).
Experiment execution and reproducibility
Runs OmniZip on both Qwen2.5-Omni-7B and Qwen2.5-Omni-3B backbones as the OmniLLM under test (Section 4.1, Section 4.2).
Executes the two reported retention-rate configurations: 45% retained ratio with ρa=0.3, ρv=0.6 and 35% retained ratio with ρa=0.4, ρv=0.7 (except ShortVid-Bench), as stated in Section 4.1.

Runs inference on an NVIDIA A6000 48GB GPU with FlashAttention enabled to reduce memory, matching the Section 4.1 hardware/optimization setup (the FastV 7B OOM case run on an H100 80G GPU as noted in Table 2).
Uses LMMs-Eval for VideoMME evaluation and the unified testing code for the remaining benchmarks, as specified in Section 4.2.
Evaluation protocol and metric correctness
Uses the overall FLOPs ratio (counting only multimodal audio+video tokens) as the metric to set pruning ratios across methods for fair comparison, as described in Section 4.1 and the Table 2 caption.
Reports the four efficiency metrics from Table 3 on WorldSense: GPU memory, prefilling time (with speedup factor), accuracy, and latency per example, all on a single A6000 GPU (Section 4.3, Table 3).

Normalizes Table 1 omnimodal QA results as percentages with the full-token baseline accuracy set to 100%, and reports the AVUT six-task (EL/OR/OM/IE/CC/CM) breakdown plus the VideoMME w/o-subtitle column (Section 4.2, Table 1).
Reports per-domain WorldSense scores across the eight domains (Tech & Science, Culture & Politics, Daily Life, Film & TV, Performance, Games, Sports, Music) with their average, as in Table 2.
Result match versus the paper's reported targets

Reproduces WorldSense Qwen2.5-Omni-7B averages within tolerance: OmniZip 45% retained = 45.9 and 35% retained = 45.3 versus Full Tokens 46.8, with OmniZip at 35% outperforming other methods running at 50% retention (Table 2, Section 4.2).
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: Reproduces the 7B efficiency targets from Table 3: 3.42× prefilling speedup (85ms vs 291ms) and 1.42× overall latency speedup at 35%, 2.51× prefill at 45%, ~10G GPU memory reduction (25G at 35% / 28G at 45% vs 35G full) (Section 4.3, Table 3).
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: Reproduces Table 1 Qwen2.5-Omni-7B at 45% retained ratio: 99.1% normalized average score and AVUTBench average of 63.0 versus full-token 64.5 (Section 4.2, Table 1).
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: Reproduces the Qwen2.5-Omni-3B results: 3.27× prefilling speedup at 35% (Table 3) and WorldSense OmniZip 45% average = 45.2 versus Full Tokens 46.4 (Table 2).
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: Confirms the OmniZip pruning step adds less than 40 ms of runtime overhead, as claimed in Section 3.5.
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: Artifact completeness and provenance
Provides a training-free, inference-time codebase (no training/fine-tuning scripts) consistent with the released repository github.com/KD-TAO/OmniZip referenced on the title page.

Includes the ablation reproductions: Table 5 isolating dynamic video pruning (DP) and audio anchor consolidation (AC) on Qwen2.5-Omni-7B, and Table 4 comparing token-selection strategies (ISTC vs DyCoke vs VisionZip GS vs Random) (Section 4.4).

Documents the full hyperparameter configuration in a config/readme artifact: ρmax=0.75, ρmin=0.35, k=5, G=15 (AVUT)/G=3 (others), and the (ρa, ρv) pairs (0.3, 0.6) at 45% and (0.4, 0.7) at 35%, matching Section 4.1.
